channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Tesla Recalling 219,000 US Vehicles for Rearview Image Issue
item.description: Tesla is recalling 218,868 vehicles in the U.S. The regulator said the rearview camera display in affected vehicles may be delayed when the car is put into reverse, reducing driver visibility. Related: NHTSA Ends Probe of Tesla Model Y Vehicles Without Manufacturer Action The recall covers certain Model 3, Model Y, Model S, and Model X vehicles, it said.

item.title: US to Adopt 25% Car Levy ‘Soon’ Unless EU Clears Trade Deal
item.description: Ambassador to the EU Andrew Puzder said Washington will implement 25% tariffs on cars and trucks from the European Union “relatively soon” if the bloc doesn’t swiftly ratify a long-delayed trade deal. “Unless we see some substantial progress, I think you probably should expect those relatively soon,” Puzder told Bloomberg Television. Puzder’s comments add pressure to EU negotiators this week as they try to finalize proposed amendments to the deal.

item.title: White House Prepares Order to Boost AI Security, Hassett Says
item.description: The White House is looking into an executive order that would create a vetting system for new artificial intelligence models like Anthropic PBC’s Mythos in a bid to protect business and government networks from AI-related cyber risks, a top economic adviser said Wednesday. The White House did not immediately respond to a request for comment on whether the executive order would mandate that AI models secure government approval prior to their release. “We’re studying, possibly an executive order to give a clear roadmap to everybody about how this is going to go and how future AIs that also potentially create vulnerabilities should go through a process so that they’re released to the wild after they’ve been proven safe, just like an FDA drug,” National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett told Fox Business.

item.title: Top Google Scientist Claims EU Data Measures Pose User Privacy Risk
item.description: A top Google scientist sent a warning to EU antitrust regulators on Tuesday that its proposal requiring the company to share search engine data with rivals such as OpenAI risked exposing users’ private information, the sternest rebuke yet in a tussle over Google’s lucrative business model. Sergei Vassilvitskii, with the title of distinguished scientist at Google since 2012 and regarded as a leader in his field, will meet EU antitrust officials on Wednesday to voice his concerns and propose a broader approach with better guardrails. The meeting comes a month after the Commission outlined a series of steps that Google should take to allow rival search engines access to search data, such as ranking, query, click, and view data on fair, reasonable, and non-discriminatory terms.

item.title: Texas Leaders Say Summer Camps Should Move Forward With 2026 Applications
item.description: “We, the leadership of the Texas Senate and Texas House, support allowing camps to qualify for licensure through the Department of State Health Services to operate for the summer 2026 season if they have submitted a sufficient emergency action plan, meet all other safety requirements, and maintain a reliable communication system capable of operating during an emergency,” the statement added. Dan Patrick and Texas House of Representatives Speaker Dustin Burrows indicated the state’s new camp safety acts, passed in response to the Camp Mystic flooding disaster, have strengthened safety standards for camps statewide, allowing camps to apply for licensing this year. In a joint statement issued yesterday, Texas Lt.

item.title: Arizona Brush Fire That Burned 1138 Acres 45% Contained
item.description: The fire is reportedly 45% contained and has burned 1138 acres, according to information provided by Wildfire.gov. The Hazen Fire that began on the evening of May 2 near Buckeye, Arizona, has reportedly begun to be contained, according to an alert by the Western Fire Chiefs Association. Several fire departments have responded to the rapidly growing wildfire fueled by salt cedar, grass, and brush.

item.title: Insurtech AkinovA hires Gilpin as Group Chief Operating Officer
item.description: AkinovA, the technology-led re/insurance platform focused on expanding access to underwriting capital for large corporate risks, including via capital markets participation, has appointed Matthew Gilpin as Group Chief Operating Officer (COO). As Group COO, Gilpin will reportedly spearhead the operational build-out required to support AkinovA’s next phase of growth, which includes governance, cross-jurisdictional operating structures, institutional readiness and execution discipline across the firm’s London and Bermuda activities. AkinovA also confirmed that Gilpin will help support the build-out of its Managing General Underwriting capabilities, technology-enabled underwriting workflows, corporate data infrastructure and capital-partner operating model.

item.title: White Mountains gets positive income from Ark’s Outrigger Re sidecar in Q1 2026
item.description: White Mountains reported that the Outrigger Re collateralized reinsurance sidecar, which supports its P&C insurance and reinsurance subsidiary Ark, continued to deliver positive income through the first-quarter of 2026, as even without having made a fresh sidecar investment for this year, benefits still flow for the company. Previously, White Mountains has reported the income generated by its stake in the Outrigger Re sidecar each quarter, but with no fresh investment made for 2026 the reporting has changed somewhat. Ark also reported, “Through March 31, 2026, WM Outrigger Re has generated pre-tax income of $57 million from the 2025 underwriting year, $29 million from the 2024 underwriting year and $76 million from the 2023 underwriting year.” Some of those figures have risen since the full-year 2025 results, when White Mountains had reported pre-tax income of $55 million attributable to the 2025 underwriting year from the Outrigger Re sidecar investment .

item.title: Hestia Re 2026-1 cat bond reinforces Kin’s commitment to protecting policyholders, Conlin
item.description: (Series 2026-1) issuance, reinforces the company’s commitment to protecting policyholders for years to come, Chief Insurance Officer Angel Conlin has said today. Kin Chief Insurance Officer Angel Conlin, commented: “This catastrophe bond reinforces our commitment to protecting policyholders for years to come. Kin managed to successfully upsize its latest Hestia Re Series 2026-1 catastrophe bond issuance from its initial $300 million size, to secure $335 million of fully-collateralized named storm reinsurance, while the pricing of the four tranches of notes were finalised below guidance in most cases.

item.title: Allianz to transfer commercial cyber insurance business to Coalition in new partnership
item.description: Allianz Commercial and Coalition, an active insurer focused on preventing digital risks, today announced a transformative, strategic agreement to expand their cyber insurance partnership. By transitioning its commercial cyber insurance business to Coalition, Allianz is combining the distinct capabilities of both companies to serve business customers worldwide. This agreement makes Coalition Allianz’s exclusive global partner for commercial cyber insurance across all segments.

item.title: BMS Group adopts mea Platform’s AI products for advance placement efficiency
item.description: BMS Group, an independent insurance and reinsurance broker, has adopted mea Platform’s broking operations products, which are a part of its operational suite, to enhance placement efficiency and drive digital transformation. Martin Henley, Chief Executive Officer, mea Platform, commented, “Placement efficiency is a defining advantage for leading brokers in today’s market. mea Platform is an AI product company serving the re/insurance sector.

item.title: Amwins acquires energy & infrastructure broker NARDAC
item.description: Amwins, a global distributor of specialty insurance products, has acquired NARDAC, a brokerage specialising in energy and infrastructure. Rob Battenfield, executive vice president at Amwins Brokerage and co-leader of Amwins Energy, Power and Infrastructure practice commented: “As the needs of the market have evolved, we are excited to add NARDAC’s expertise to our practice, creating more options for our retail partners to access world class solutions. According to the announcement, NARDAC’s specialist wholesale, reinsurance and underwriting services will be integrated into Amwins’ existing Energy practice.

item.title: Allianz Commercial Transitions its Standalone Cyber Business to MGA Coalition
item.description: Allianz Commercial announced an agreement which transitions its standalone commercial cyber insurance business to Coalition, the managing general agent and specialist cyber insurer. The agreement establishes Coalition as Allianz’s exclusive global partner for cyber insurance across all commercial segments, combining the strengths of both companies for business customers around the world. Upon completion, Coalition will take primary responsibility for pricing, product development, risk mitigation, and claims management for Allianz’s standalone commercial cyber portfolio, supported by Allianz resources for multinational and large corporate underwriting, service delivery, and claims.

item.title: People Moves: Brotherhood Mutual Announces Leadership Promotions & Transition; Assurex Global’s Johnson Named SVP, Global Growth Strategy
item.description: Brotherhood Mutual Announces Leadership Promotions & Transition Brotherhood Mutual Insurance Company , headquartered in Fort Wayne, Indiana, made several changes to its executive team. Assurex Global’s Johnson Named SVP, Global Growth Strategy Assurex Global , headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, named Troy Johnson senior vice president, global growth strategy. Before joining Brotherhood Mutual, he held leadership roles in both sales and underwriting for Protective Insurance (formerly Baldwin & Lyons).

item.title: People Moves: NCDOI Names Price Deputy Commissioner Over Company Services
item.description: North Carolina’s insurance commissioner has named a financial analysis veteran to the role of senior deputy commissioner, overseeing the Company Services Group at the Department of Insurance. The Company Services Group includes the DOI’s Financial Analysis Division, the Financial Examinations Division, and Financial Oversight and Special Entities divisions, Commissioner Mike Causey said in a bulletin. Jessica Price has led financial analysis at NCDOI for 15 years, and before that, was a tax auditor at the state Department of Revenue.

item.title: Gallagher unveils AI-powered Blueprint to optimise insurance programmes
item.description: Gallagher has launched Gallagher Blueprint, a new AI-driven framework aimed at helping clients improve their risk profiles and design more cost-effective insurance programmes. The Blueprint combines advanced analytics with Gallagher’s proprietary data and sector-specific expertise to create a structured approach to insurance planning. The company says the framework delivers fast, actionable recommendations, giving clients greater clarity over their coverage and helping ensure their insurance programmes are aligned with broader business objectives.

item.title: Uninsured Driver Problem Isn't What You Think
item.description: Non-standard auto insurers' fee structures may be producing the very uninsured population they're designed to avoid. But it is incomplete in one critical respect: it treats the uninsured rate as something that happens to the insurance industry, rather than something the insurance industry has, in meaningful part, produced. Picture a driver who has been paying premiums faithfully for months.

item.title: Kin secures $335M catastrophe bond
item.description: Kin secured a $335 million catastrophe bond through Hestia Re Ltd, marking the company’s largest reinsurance transaction to date and its fourth CAT bond issuance. The multi-year deal includes four bond tranches and, for the first time, extends coverage beyond Florida to additional states where Kin operates. The CAT bond provides protection against major weather events including hurricanes and other catastrophic storms.

item.title: 🦄 Corgi raises $160 million
item.description: Business insurance startup Corgi has raised $160 million in Series B funding at a $1.3 billion valuation. This financing builds on Corgi’s previously announced $108 million across its seed and Series A rounds, backed by investors including Y Combinator, Kindred Ventures, Contrary, and more. The new capital will support the continued expansion of Corgi’s startup insurance products, including broader coverage, deeper distribution, and ongoing investment in the AI systems that power underwriting, claims, and policy operations.

item.title: iA Financial reshuffles executive team to support growth strategy
item.description: iA Financial Group announced executive committee changes effective June 1, 2026, including the creation of a new role focused on acquisition-driven growth and capital deployment. The changes are intended to support long-term growth, operational efficiency, and its acquisition strategy. Benoit Hudon joined the company as Executive Vice-President, Corporate Strategy and Development, where he will help lead strategy, performance, and acquisitions.

item.title: Las primas medias de nueva producción en No Vida caen en abril, salvo en Motos
item.description: La prima media de nueva producción en los principales ramos de No Vida registró en abril un descenso generalizado , según los datos del multitarificador Avant2 Sales Manager, analizados mediante tecnología de Versus Analytics. Los seguros de Autos de nueva producción experimentaron un descenso del 2% en abril, tanto en términos interanuales como intermensuales, situándose la prima media en 459 euros. En Hogar , la prima media de nueva producción descendió un 1% interanual.

item.title: Deadly China fireworks blast triggers US$878,000 work safety payout from Ping An P&C
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: AIG hires Ivy Thoo as senior casualty underwriter
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Competition Act 1998 investigations
item.description: Following the publication of financial reporting by PayPal Holdings Inc, we can confirm we are investigating Mastercard, PayPal and Visa under Chapter I in the Competition Act 1998, and Mastercard and Visa under Chapter II in the Competition Act 1998, for suspected anti-competitive conduct linked to the funding and usage of PayPal’s digital wallet. The FCA has reached no conclusions nor made any findings with regard to competition law having been broken.

item.title: Frontier Labs And Robotics Companies Again Top List Of New Unicorns In April
item.description: A total of 28 companies joined The Crunchbase Unicorn Board in April, Crunchbase data shows, with robotics startups and frontier labs leading by number of entrants for the second consecutive month . Six companies working on humanoid robotics — five from China and one from Japan — also received billion-dollar-plus valuations last month. The financial services, defense, developer tools, energy and healthcare sectors each added two or three new unicorns in April.

item.title: AI Is Rewriting What Investors Should Look For In Early Startup Teams
item.description: Every generation of technology resets what investors should expect from founders. Investors are shifting their attention from what a team can build to whether the founder has domain expertise that predates the startup, has done real customer discovery, and can articulate a path to market that competitors can’t easily replicate. It has implications for how early teams should be composed.

item.title: People’s Trust targets mid-guidance pricing for debut $100m GWS Re cat bond
item.description: People’s Trust Insurance Company, the Florida homeowners specialist insurer, is now targeting mid-guidance pricing for the $100 million of named storm reinsurance it aims to secure from the capital markets through its debut GWS Re Limited (Series 2026-1) catastrophe bond issuance, Artemis can report. Last month, People’s Trust Insurance Company became the latest new sponsor to make its debut in the catastrophe bond market. As a result, it appears that People’s Trust Insurance Company is looking to capitalise on cat bond investor demand, and favourable market conditions, to secure its first catastrophe bond coverage at attractive pricing.

item.title: Allstate returns with $200m target for Florida-focused Sanders Re III 2026-2 cat bond
item.description: This is Allstate’s second visit to the catastrophe bond market of 2026, having sponsored its largest ever single visit to the cat bond market in March when it secured $1.2 billion of nationwide (ex-Florida) reinsurance from two Sanders cat bond issuances . This new Florida-focused cat bond sponsorship takes the number of Sanders Re deals we’ve seen from Allstate to twenty-five, while it makes now twenty-seven cat bonds in total that we’ve tracked and analysed from the insurer. Allstate has a $300 million Class A tranche of notes from its Sanders Re III 2023-2 Florida-focused cat bond that is scheduled to mature this June.

item.title: Fuse expands real-time peril intelligence platform Watch to global markets
item.description: Fuse International, an AI-powered commercial insurance intelligence platform, has announced the global expansion of Watch, a live peril dashboard designed to streamline risk management by consolidating global catastrophe data into a single workspace. “Watch consolidates the world’s authoritative catastrophe data sources into one workspace and, when paired with a synced book of business, computes total insured value (TIV) at risk for every event as it unfolds — naming the affected insureds, the carriers on those layers, and the aggregate exposure across the book in near real time,” Fuse explained. A key advantage of the platform to highlight, is the ability for companies to sync their portfolio data for real-time insights into how unfolding catastrophes may impact their specific book of business.

item.title: Atrium appoints Naveed Haque as Head of Casualty Reinsurance
item.description: Atrium, a specialty insurer at Lloyd’s, has announced the appointment of Naveed Haque as Head of Casualty Reinsurance. In this role at Atrium, he will be responsible for building and developing the company’s Casualty Reinsurance portfolio, with an emphasis on sustainable growth and disciplined underwriting performance within Atrium’s wider casualty offering. Naveed is a highly respected leader with a deep understanding of the Casualty reinsurance market.

item.title: IGI’s underwriting income rises to $37.7m as CoR improves to 89.1% in Q1’26
item.description: (IGI), a specialist commercial insurer and reinsurer, has reported underwriting income of $37.7 million for the first quarter of 2026, up 35.1% from $27.9 million in the same period a year earlier, with an improved combined ratio of 89.1% compared to 94.4%. IGI attributed the improvement in underwriting income to net premiums earned of $111.2 million, partially offset by net loss and loss adjustment expenses of $54.8 million. Waleed Jabsheh, Group President & CEO of IGI, said, “We had a strong start to 2026 highlighted by underwriting income of $37.7 million and an 89.1% combined ratio, driven by consistent and disciplined execution.

item.title: Fuse unveils global expansion of real-time peril layer Watch
item.description: Commercial insurance intelligence platform Fuse has expanded the scope of Watch, its real-time peril intelligence layer built for the entire insurance value chain. “Watch consolidates the world’s authoritative catastrophe data sources into one workspace and, when paired with a synced book of business, computes total insured value (TIV) at risk for every event as it unfolds — naming the affected insureds, the carriers on those layers, and the aggregate exposure across the book in near real time,” Fuse explained. Fuse announced the general availability of Watch in April this year , unveiling a product that plots every active US peril on a single live map, purpose-built for commercial insurance brokers and underwriters.

item.title: Spanberger Weighs Legislation to Allow Class Action Lawsuits in Virginia Courts
item.description: Abigail Spanberger is weighing whether to sign or veto legislation that would, for the first time, allow Virginians to file class action lawsuits in state courts — a shift advocates say would expand access to justice, but one that has drawn pushback over how far the change should go. At its core, the legislation would allow one or more individuals to represent a larger group in court if certain conditions are met, including that the class is so large that individual lawsuits would be impractical, that claims share common legal or factual questions, and that the named plaintiff can actually represent the group’s interests. Virginia is one of only two states, along with Mississippi, that does not provide a formal class action procedure in its state court system.

item.title: Insurance Distribution Associations Unite to Form New Industry Advisory Council
item.description: ACORD, the global standards-setting body for the insurance industry, announced the formation of the ACORD Inter-Association Advisory Council (IAAC). IAAC is a coalition of North American property/casualty associations representing independent agents, brokers, and intermediaries across the North American distribution channel. The inaugural meeting on May 4 included leaders from ACORD, AUGIE Group (an insurance tech exchange), the Council of Insurance Agents & Brokers (CIAB), the Centre for Study of Insurance Operations (CSIO), the Independent Insurance Agents & Brokers of America/Agents Council for Technology (IIABA/ACT), the Professional Insurance Agents (PIA National), and Wholesale & Specialty Insurance Association (WSIA).

item.title: Musk Agrees to Pay $1.5 Million Over SEC Twitter Stake Case
item.description: Elon Musk agreed to pay $1.5 million to settle Securities and Exchange Commission allegations that he cheated Twitter shareholders in 2022 by failing to properly disclose his growing stake in the social media company. The agency in December 2024 asked Musk to pay more than $200 million to settle, according to a letter his lawyers sent to the agency and reviewed by Bloomberg News. That delay cost Twitter shareholders more than $150 million, the regulator said .

item.title: Aventum enters MGA market with acquisition of marine specialist Post & Co
item.description: Specialty re/insurance group Aventum has completed its first managing general agent (MGA) acquisition, purchasing marine underwriting specialist Post & Co as part of its strategy to build a leading independent specialty MGA platform. The acquisition also enhances the capabilities of Aventum’s global managing general specialist Rokstone, bringing the two businesses together under one platform. In welcoming Roderick and his team into the Aventum family, we gain not only a business of the highest calibre, but a leadership group whose experience and standing in the European MGA market will be invaluable.” Roderick Post, CEO of Post & Co, added: “Post & Co has been part of our family for more than 100 years, and the decision to find the right long-term partner was one we approached with considerable care.

item.title: Blitzy secures $200m funding with backing from major insurance investors
item.description: Blitzy has raised $200 million in a new funding round, reaching a valuation of $1.4 billion, with the round led by Northzone and supported by investors including Battery Ventures and PSG. Strategic participation also came from insurance-focused investors Liberty Mutual Strategic Ventures, Erie Strategic Ventures, and BAL Ventures, highlighting growing insurer interest in AI-driven development platforms. Blitzy plans to use the new funding to accelerate research and development, expand hiring, and deepen partnerships, particularly in highly regulated sectors where demand for secure, scalable AI solutions continues to grow.

item.title: Two insurers back Blitzy
item.description: Blitzy raised $200 million at a $1.4 billion valuation in a funding round led by Northzone , with participation from investors including Battery Ventures , PSG , and strategic backers Liberty Mutual Strategic Ventures , Erie Strategic Ventures , and BAL Ventures . Founded by 2023 by Brian Elliott and Sid Pardeshi, Blitzy develops autonomous software development tools for enterprises. Blitzy (a team of ~85) is used by Global 2000 companies across 10 industries, including insurance and financial services, and plans to use the funding to expand research, hiring, and partnerships in regulated sectors.

item.title: Fuse Takes Watch Global, Delivering Real-Time Peril Intelligence Across Five Regions for Brokers, Carriers, and Reinsurers
item.description: The post Fuse Takes Watch Global, Delivering Real-Time Peril Intelligence Across Five Regions for Brokers, Carriers, and Reinsurers appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . NEW YORK — May 6, 2026 — Fuse, the commercial insurance intelligence platform, today announced the global expansion of Watch , a real-time peril intelligence layer built for the entire insurance value chain. Watch is the layer that closes those gaps — the same gaps every part of the value chain has been working around for years.” Global Coverage at Launch Regions: Watch is live across five macro regions and twenty-two sub-region scopes: Americas — United States, Canada, Latin America, All Americas Europe — UK & Ireland, Western Europe, Nordics, Southern Europe, Central & Eastern Europe, All Europe Asia-Pacific — Japan, Australia & New Zealand, Southeast Asia, South Asia, Greater China, All APAC Africa & MEA — GCC / Gulf, North Africa, East Africa, West Africa, Southern Africa, All MEA Global — Worldwide satellite view sitting over the regional layers Perils: Watch tracks ten peril classes continuously — earthquake, tropical cyclone (hurricane / typhoon), flood, wildfire, volcano, drought, severe convective storm (wind / hail / tornado), winter storms, heatwave, and coastal and marine hazards.

item.title: Firmado el Convenio de Seguros para 2025-2028
item.description: Unespa, Amat, Asecore y la Confederación Española de Mutualidades, junto a CCOO-Servicios y UGT, han firmado en Madrid el nuevo convenio colectivo sectorial que, con vigencia entre 2025 y 2028, se aplicará a unas 75.000 personas. Para el periodo 2026-2028 se mantiene la fórmula de revisión salarial vinculada a la evolución del PIB , corregida por el IPC, incorporando además nuevos tramos en sus parámetros para ajustar la actualización a las posibles variaciones de ambos indicadores durante la vigencia del convenio. La compensación por comida se incrementará de forma progresiva, pasando de 11,30 euros en 2025 a 13 euros en 2027 y 2028.

item.title: «Un buen líder debe ser íntegro, empático y capaz de decidir en momentos críticos»
item.description: Flavia Rodríguez-Ponga, directora general del Consorcio de Compensación de Seguros, participó la semana pasada en la Masterclass del curso de liderazgo femenino Next Generation Women in Insurance, impulsado por #RedEWI, donde compartió con las alumnas una reflexión sobre liderazgo, trayectoria profesional, igualdad y el papel de la mujer en el sector asegurador.

item.title: Verisk Integrates Analytics Claude AI Platform
item.description: Verisk (Jersey City, N.J.) has integrated its analytics into Anthropic ’s (San Francisco) Claude platform through Model Context Protocol connectors, enabling underwriting and claims workflows within a generative artificial intelligence environment for the property/casualty market. “By pairing Claude with Verisk’s governed analytics and established controls, this collaboration shows how generative AI can enhance professional decision-making without compromising the rigor and accountability the industry demands.” Verisk says the connectors are designed to support integration of data and analytics into enterprise artificial intelligence environments across insurance workflows. The connectors provide access to Verisk’s data and analytics through natural language queries, allowing users to retrieve insights within existing workflows.

item.title: Apple Settles Lawsuit Over Late Siri AI Features For $250 Million
item.description: Apple on Tuesday settled for $250 million a shareholder lawsuit brought after the company delayed artificial-intelligence upgrades to its Siri voice assistant. In 2025, Apple said that the AI overhaul of Siri would not come until this year, and executives have now confirmed that the new Siri features will be unveiled at Apple’s annual developer conference next month. In a statement, Apple said it released numerous other AI features since the launch of what it calls Apple Intelligence in 2024.

item.title: FAA Proposes Limits on Drone Flights to Address Security Worries
item.description: The Trump administration is proposing rules to tighten security against rogue drone flights. The proposal comes as President Donald Trump takes other steps to address unauthorized drone flights around sporting events, especially as the nation prepares to host the 2026 FIFA World Cup and 2028 Summer Olympics. The rules, released Tuesday, require applicants to explain why a restriction is necessary for “aviation safety, protection of people and property on the ground, national security, or homeland security.” That includes describing drone operations over the facility during the previous 24 months, highlighting any gaps or weaknesses that might be exploited and detailing the consequences of a potential incursion.

item.title: Berkshire Taps Gen Re’s Shamieh to Succeed Jain as Insurance Chief, WSJ reports
item.description: Berkshire Hathaway has selected Gen Re Chairman Charlie Shamieh as successor to longtime insurance leader Ajit Jain, the Wall Street Journal reported on Monday, citing people familiar with the matter. Shamieh, a veteran of the insurance industry, is set to take over Berkshire’s vast insurance operations once 74-year-old Jain retires, the report said. Berkshire owns reinsurer Gen Re, where Shamieh has served as chairman since 2018.

item.title: Sedgwick Australia appoints Simon Kay as CEO, Kimberley Daley as COO
item.description: Sedgwick Australia has appointed Simon Kay as CEO and Kimberley Daley as COO, the global loss adjuster said a press release. Your company may already have a corporate license in place.

item.title: AIG moves Igor Chichkan to Singapore as APAC head of financial lines
item.description: Your company may already have a corporate license in place.

item.title: New Zealand faces ‘pockets of vulnerability’ from rising home insurance costs: RBNZ
item.description: Please Login The post New Zealand faces ‘pockets of vulnerability’ from rising home insurance costs: RBNZ appeared first on InsuranceAsia News . Your company may already have a corporate license in place.

item.title: Report on Vulnerabilities in Private Credit
item.description: This report provides an overview of the private credit ecosystem, identifying potential vulnerabilities related to interlinkages with banks. While the growth of private credit may bring benefits, it also affects potential vulnerabilities. Private credit at its current size and scope has not been tested during a severe economic downturn, which could expose leverage and borrower credit quality vulnerabilities.

item.title: FSB warns on private credit vulnerabilities
item.description: Press enquiries: +41 61 280 8477 [email protected] Ref: 7/2026 The Financial Stability Board (FSB) today published a report on financial stability vulnerabilities in private credit . Notwithstanding the benefits private credit brings, in the form of tailored finance for companies and diversification for investors, it also embeds several vulnerabilities. The Financial Stability Board (FSB) today published a report on financial stability vulnerabilities in private credit.
